name: aligned_zero_size
reason: If alignment with size 0 is satisfiable, it masks division-by-zero in any spec that relies on aligned without guarding size > 0

name: inv_empty_arch
reason: An architecture with zero layers is vacuously valid under inv, which would allow meaningless architectures to satisfy all downstream specs

name: upper_vaddr_no_progress
code:
proof fn phi_3_upper_vaddr_no_progress(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.upper_vaddr(layer, base) <= base,
{
}
reason: If upper_vaddr never exceeds base, the layer covers zero address space, making all entry-size and mapping reasoning unsound

name: entry_base_collision
code:
proof fn phi_4_entry_base_collision(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, i: nat, j: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        i < arch.num_entries(layer),
        j < arch.num_entries(layer),
        i != j,
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, i) == arch.entry_base(layer, base, j),
{
}
reason: Distinct entry indices mapping to the same base address would collapse the entire page table structure, making separate pages indistinguishable

name: aligned_implies_double_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_implies_double_aligned(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
        aligned(addr, size),
    ensures
        aligned(addr, 2 * size),
{
}
reason: Alignment to size does not imply alignment to 2*size; if provable it would mean every 4K-aligned address is also 8K-aligned, breaking page-size distinctions
